explanation: This LTL formula captures the requirement that the robot should continuously provide an explanation of its current location as part of its behavior in the environment.

This LTL formula describes that whenever the robot is located, it should continuously provide explanations on why it believes it is situated that way, fulfilling the task of communicating its understanding of its position.
The LTL formula ensures that globally, whenever the robot returns or indicates its current location, it is also accompanied by an explanation for that position and its validity.
The property ensures that any time the robot attempts to express its location, it also supplies an explanation for its reasoning, ensuring transparency in robot operation.
This LTL property ensures that any time the robot gives its current location, it follows up with detailed explanations, thereby ensuring full transparency and reasoning.
The LTL formula implies that whenever the robot believes it knows its current position, it must provide reasons whenever it acknowledges or communicates this belief.
This template mandates that if the robot believes or indicates it knows where it is (i.e., robotLocation is true), it must continuously supply reasoning or explanations as to why.
This formula captures the necessity for the robot to provide explanations every time it acknowledges or reports its location, such as when follow-up decisions or actions depend on this information.
This LTL formula ensures that anytime the robot's current location is reported, it follows up with providing comprehensive reasons, adhering to the need for informative transparency.
Whenever the robot returns its current location, it must also provide detailed reasoning regularly, ensuring explanations complement all positions communicated.
When a robot is fulfilling its task to report location, it must provide detailed explanations to contextualize its self-awareness and location cognition.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) | (X ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))

explanation: This requirement demands that whenever the robot relays its position, it follows up with an explanation for transparency and clarity.
If the robot shares its location, it must explain the reasoning, ensuring all location information is supported with logic to justify its belief.
The property ensures that every expression of the robot's location by the system is always followed by justified explanation of the rationale behind that information.
Checking that every reported location from the robot includes a reason or explanation provides assurance in decision transparency and operational clarity.
Providing location reports with accompanying logic or rationale ensures the explanation method always aligns with the actions taken by the robot concerning its position.
This template ensures every location record made by the robot in monitoring the mission is validated by supportive reasons, aligning with the proactive approach.

The robot should continuously explain its location using 'provideReason', while also providing its current location knowledge through 'robotLocation'.

Globally, if the information is requested using 'infoRequest', the robot should explain its location using 'explainLocation'.
While operating, the robot must explain its current location within a bounded time frame.

If there's a situation assessment, ensure an explanation is available for the robot's perceived location consistently.
The robot should eventually return its location with an explanation as to why it thinks it is there.
Any time an info request is active with an ability to provide details, ensure an explanation for locations is given.
Every time the robot details a task or path, it should provide a detailed explanation regarding its location.
At any given time, when detailed reasoning is feasible, the robot should share an explanation for its present location.
If the task completion probability changes, always explain why the robot thinks it's at its current location.
The system should always justify the robot's location in the context of current path information.
If misalignment is detected regarding planned and intended paths, ensure a rational location explanation is given.
Respond to unexpected conditions by enabling the robot to explain its perceived location globally.
